comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking). Remediating the 1 advisory (caller migration for the compute_outbound_mutations signature change): ALL call sites are IN SCOPE for this PR. Production: reconcile.py:928-937 (only prod caller; reads absent_alive_fields out-param after the call at ~1005-1006). Tests: test_outbound_differ.py, test_outbound_differ_bound_but_absent.py (uses out-param), test_roundtrip.py, test_binding_commit_survives_merge.py, + others. All migrate to OutboundDiffConfig + the (mutations, absent_alive_fields) return tuple in the same change; no backward-compat shim (forward-only, internal API).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Scope decision (user-approved): the comment-diff seam alone removes only ~245 LOC (1545->1301), which cannot reach the AC's <800 target. Expanding to the FULL per-concern split along the existing logical seams: comment-diff -> outbound_comments.py (done), field-diff -> outbound_fields.py, link-diff -> outbound_links.py. outbound_differ.py becomes the orchestrator (compute_outbound_mutations + OutboundDiffConfig) + a re-export facade so outbound_differ.<name> keeps resolving for the test suite (zero test churn except the compute_outbound_mutations signature migration). This MEETS the original <800 AC + removes the allowlist entry.

compiled_state.description:
RISK 2 (over-cap growth). `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` grew +268 LOC to 1545 — now the largest file in the tree — and is allowlisted, so CI cannot stop the accretion. Split it along the EXISTING logical seam (the comment-diff cluster), not by line count.

## Why
The prior audit's explicit watch-item materialized here. `compute_outbound_mutations` is also a 320-LOC body with 9 params incl. a mutable `absent_alive_fields` out-param.

## What — careful split along the comment-diff seam
- Extract the cohesive comment-diff cluster to a NEW sibling `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_comments.py` (~250-300 LOC, clears the 100-LOC floor): `_diff_comments` (`:795-916`) + its satellites `_normalize_comment_body`, `_decorate_outbound_comment`, `_map_comments_for_create`, `_is_machine_marker_comment`, `_load_comment_limits`, `_load_adf` (per `docs/architecture.md:216`).
- Keep field/label/link differs + `compute_outbound_mutations` (the orchestrator) in `outbound_differ.py`; it imports the comment module.
- Fold in the smell fix: introduce an `OutboundDiffConfig` dataclass for the optional flags (`excluded_statuses`, `local_label_intent`, `client`, `pass_id`, `prev_snapshot`) and RETURN `(mutations, absent_alive_fields)` instead of the out-param.

## Scope
In: comment-cluster extraction + the config-object/return-tuple refactor; preserve the idempotent diff contract exactly. Out: changing what mutations are computed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `outbound_comments.py` created (>100 LOC) holding the comment-diff cluster; `outbound_differ.py` imports it.
- [ ] `outbound_differ.py` drops BELOW 800 LOC and is REMOVED from `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`.
- [ ] `compute_outbound_mutations` takes an `OutboundDiffConfig` (no more 9 positional params) and returns `(mutations, absent_alive_fields)` (no mutable out-param).
- [ ] Reconciler diff tests pass byte-for-byte; no behavior change to outbound mutations.
compiled_state.title: Risk 2: split outbound_differ.py along the comment-diff seam
